reason: Response B has a more straightforward, familiar motivational quote structure that plays on the classic "every expert was once a beginner" trope, adding a tongue-in-cheek twist with "refused to admit they had no idea what they were doing." This kind of accessible, relatable humor with a clear setup and punchline feels more characteristic of Sonnet's style. Response A is more terse and cryptic with a darker edge ("corners cut back"), which feels more like the compressed, literary wit one might expect from Opus.
